summary: Excellent early‑80s show with standout performances of Candyman, Tennessee Jed and The Other One; recording quality not discussed but presumed adequate.
review: Fans rave about this early‑80s concert, highlighting a hot first set featuring Candyman, Tennessee Jed and Peggy‑O and a blistering second set with Scarlet Begonias segueing into Fire. Bob Weir’s LL>Supplication and The Other One are singled out as gems, and Jerry Garcia’s playing is praised throughout. No specific audio commentary was provided, so the tape is assumed to be listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong, enthusiastic praise for the musical performance across three reviews; lack of technical detail keeps recording assessment modest, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers did not comment on soundboard vs. audience or clarity; assumed to be acceptable for listening.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as a hot, Jerry‑focused opener; second set built momentum with Scarlet Begonias and an explosive jam into Fire, creating a high‑energy arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s early‑80s playing is lauded, especially on Candyman and other first‑set numbers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob shines on the LL>Supplication sequence and The Other One, which reviewers call gems.
